# .claude/project-scope.md
# Generated by /team-brief discovery step. Update manually if project scope changes.
# Read by: /team-design (Step 1), /team-plan (Step 1), /team-build (optional worktree), /team-qa (routing), /team-tdd (domain rows).
# generated_at: 2026-04-24

domains:
  - agentic-systems  # primary — multi-agent orchestrator, per-session containers, MCP tools, agent-to-agent routing
  - llm-engineering  # secondary — wraps @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk + pluggable providers (Codex, OpenCode)

primary_language: typescript
framework: node-host + bun-container  # split runtime; host on Node/pnpm, agent container on Bun
test_framework: vitest + bun-test    # vitest on host, bun:test in container

relevant_global_skills:
  - agentic-systems
  - llm-engineering

description: >
  NanoClaw v2 — personal Claude assistant that orchestrates per-session agent containers.
  Host (Node + pnpm) writes inbound messages to a per-session SQLite DB; container
  (Bun + agent-runner) polls, calls a provider (Claude Agent SDK / Codex / OpenCode),
  and writes outbound messages back. Two-DB session split (inbound.db, outbound.db)
  is the only IO surface between host and container. Channel adapters and non-default
  providers ship as install skills from sibling branches (channels/, providers/),
  not on trunk. MCP tools live in container/agent-runner/src/mcp-tools/ and surface
  capabilities like create_agent, send_message, scheduling, self-mod, git worktrees.
